nativeRange.habitat: Found on reef-associated rubble slopes at the base of vertical cliffs, typically at depths of 22 to 50 metres in the Western Pacific.

diet.notes: Expected to feed on small benthic invertebrates and zooplankton, consistent with other benthic Trimma gobies. Has been known to accept small frozen foods such as copepods, Mysis shrimp, and enriched brine shrimp in captivity, though targeted feeding may be needed.

avoidWith: predatory fish that consume small gobies

summary: A tiny, vividly marked pygmy goby from the Western Pacific, generally considered suitable for nano and species-oriented reef aquariums. Tends to hover close to the substrate near rubble and rock, displaying striking red spots across its body.
careNotes: The Red Spotted Goby is a rarely encountered species in the aquarium trade, native to the Western Pacific where it inhabits rubble slopes at the base of vertical cliffs at depths of roughly 22 to 50 metres. In the wild, individuals typically hover vertically or at an oblique angle just a few centimetres above the substrate. Maximum recorded length is 3.5 cm. Aquarium care guidance is largely extrapolated from general reef goby husbandry and the closely related Trimma okinawae, as this species has limited documented captive history. Water parameters and husbandry requirements are expected to follow standard reef conditions. A tightly fitting lid is strongly advised, as gobies — including Trimma species — are widely known to jump. Feeding tends to be moderately challenging; small live or frozen copepods and Mysis shrimp are a reasonable starting point, and targeted feeding may be necessary to ensure the fish receives adequate nutrition. Its naturally deep habitat suggests a preference for dimmer lighting and stable, consistent water conditions. The species is assessed as Least Concern by the IUCN (August 2020).

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species not listed across all goby collection pages. Gobies collection browsed across multiple pages; Trimma rubromaculatum not found. LiveAquaria effectively could not serve as primary source for this species.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Primary data source used. Confirmed: scientific name, family (Gobiidae), order (Gobiiformes), max size (3.5 cm SL), temperature range (27.7–28.5°C), depth range (22–50 m), Western Pacific distribution, reef-associated rubble habitat, IUCN Least Concern. Swapped to de facto primary since LiveAquaria did not carry this species. pH and salinity values extrapolated from standard reef parameters and Trimma okinawae comparison.
